description: Refactor the unpublished package onto domain-shaped entrypoints, generated export maps, and an unflattened src/internal layout before first npm and JSR release.

tasks.title: Map canonical symbol ownership
tasks.description:
Inventory the current root and subpath exports and assign every public symbol one owning domain. Done when:
- every public symbol is mapped to `x509`, `verify`, `revocation`, `pkcs`, `keys`, `pem`, or `result`
- each current subpath symbol is marked keep, root-only re-export, internalize, or drop
- the ownership matrix captures high-risk renames and removals before file moves begin

tasks.title: Scaffold public and internal layout
tasks.description:
Create the target domain folders and explicit internal tree without changing behavior. Done when:
- `src/x509`, `src/verify`, `src/revocation`, `src/pkcs`, `src/keys`, `src/pem`, `src/result`, and `src/internal` exist
- local import aliases support explicit internal imports for tests and implementation code
- barrel and import-direction rules are documented near the codebase
- existing code still builds before the large moves start

tasks.title: Move helper and engine modules under internal
tasks.description:
Re-home implementation-only files under `src/internal/**` and rewrite imports downward. Done when:
- ASN.1, crypto helpers, shared helpers, extension helpers, and verification engines live under `src/internal/**`
- public leaf modules import internals directly instead of public barrels
- no public entrypoint maps to helper modules moved under `src/internal/**`
- typecheck and build still pass after the move set

tasks.title: Create the x509 public domain
tasks.description:
Move certificate, CSR, parse, extension, and name APIs into the `x509` domain and make it their canonical public home. Done when:
- `src/x509/index.ts` is the exhaustive advanced entrypoint for cert, CSR, parse, name, and extension symbols
- file ownership for `certificate`, `csr`, `extensions`, `name`, and `parse` is consolidated under `x509`
- public types in this area stop depending on cross-domain incidental ownership
- docs/examples for these workflows can point to one stable domain module

tasks.title: Create the verify public domain
tasks.description:
Move verify, identity, policy, and name-constraints APIs into the `verify` domain and make it their canonical public home. Done when:
- `src/verify/index.ts` owns advanced path-validation imports
- identity, policy, and name-constraint types stop pretending to be standalone top-level products
- verify-facing public signatures resolve within the domain without export sprawl
- verify modules do not import root or sibling public barrels

tasks.title: Create the revocation public domain
tasks.description:
Merge CRL, OCSP, and revocation orchestration into one `revocation` public domain. Done when:
- `src/revocation/index.ts` owns CRL, OCSP, and revocation orchestration APIs
- the former `crl` and `ocsp` surfaces become subdomain implementation files rather than first-class package products
- cross-links to verify and x509 types resolve through domain ownership rather than accidental file-level exports
- advanced docs for revocation have one obvious import home

tasks.title: Create the pkcs public domain
tasks.description:
Merge PFX, PKCS#7, and PKCS#12 MAC support under one `pkcs` public domain. Done when:
- `src/pkcs/index.ts` is the public home for PFX and PKCS#7 workflows
- PKCS#12 MAC remains reachable only as advanced PKCS plumbing, not as a headline surface
- PKCS modules stop exporting file-shaped product seams
- docs can teach container workflows without fragmenting imports across multiple subpaths

tasks.title: Finalize keys, pem, and result domains
tasks.description:
Make `keys`, `pem`, and `result` clean standalone domains with explicit ownership rules. Done when:
- `keys`, `pem`, and `result` each have a stable public entrypoint and internal dependencies flow downward
- `pem` stays a permanent public PKI boundary module
- shared result primitives are owned only by `result` and re-exported elsewhere intentionally
- these utility domains no longer depend on accidental top-level layout assumptions

tasks.title: Redesign the root barrel around workflows
tasks.description:
Turn `micro509` into a curated workflow-first entrypoint instead of an exhaustive mirror of file exports. Done when:
- root exports the common certificate, parsing, verification, revocation, key, and PKCS workflows
- advanced type soup is trimmed back to what normal consumers actually need at root
- every root re-export points to one canonical owner domain
- root docs/examples read like guided workflows rather than a dump of every public type

tasks.title: Generate npm and JSR exports from one entry map
tasks.description:
Replace the hand-maintained export maps with one canonical entrypoint definition. Done when:
- the public domain entrypoints are defined once in `tsdown.config.ts` or a tiny shared entry-map module
- npm exports are emitted from the build configuration
- `jsr.json` exports are generated from the same entry map instead of hand-maintained
- `./package.json` remains npm-only and JSR export parity is testable

tasks.title: Migrate tests and docs to the new surface
tasks.description:
Update internal imports, examples, and docs to the new domain-shaped API. Done when:
- tests that need internals import through an explicit `#micro509/internal/*` path
- stale references to dropped file-level subpaths are removed from source docs and guides
- root-first examples and advanced domain examples both point at the new entrypoints
- PKCS#12 MAC is documented only in advanced PKCS material

tasks.title: Run packaging and doc-surface validation
tasks.description:
Validate that the refactor leaves one coherent publishable API. Done when:
- `bun typecheck`, `bun build`, and the relevant test suites pass
- `deno doc --lint` passes for root and each surviving public domain entrypoint
- `deno publish --dry-run` succeeds with the generated JSR export map
- an automated parity check proves npm and JSR exports cannot silently drift
